_meta.description: W1 Eldertree Village NPC dialogue. Forest-edge village with dying sacred trees.
_meta.author: cowir-story

forest_keeper.description: Guardian of the sacred trees. Exhausted. Watching the forest she's sworn to protect die from the inside.

lines.speaker: forest_keeper
lines.text: The trees are dying. Not the way trees die — slowly, seasonally, with the dignity of something that's had its time. They're dying from the inside. The sap runs black. The leaves fall upward.

lines.speaker: forest_keeper
lines.text: It comes from the east. From the capital's direction. We sent word. No one came.

lines.speaker: forest_keeper
lines.text: If you're heading through the forest — the corruption is thickest near the center. There's something in there. Something that was put there deliberately. The trees told me that much before they stopped talking.

lines.speaker: forest_keeper
lines.text: The trees are breathing again. Not fully — the damage will take seasons to heal. But they're breathing. Whatever you did in there, whatever you fought — it loosened the grip.

lines.speaker: forest_keeper
lines.text: You fought someone in there, didn't you? Someone with a sword and a way of looking at you like she knew exactly what you were worth.

lines.speaker: forest_keeper
lines.text: The trees remember her. They say she was honest. They say honest things are the hardest to fight.

refugee.description: Fled the capital. Speaks too quickly, as though words might be taken from him.

lines.speaker: refugee
lines.text: I came from Scriptura. The capital. Don't go there. Don't — listen, don't go there.

lines.speaker: refugee
lines.text: The chancellor has something in the tower. Not a weapon. Not a monster. Something that measures. It watches the whole kingdom and it measures what it sees and it adjusts things based on what it measures.

lines.speaker: refugee
lines.text: I worked in the filing office. I saw the reports. The cave creatures, the forest corruption, the trade routes closing — none of it is random. It's all calibrated. Someone is tuning the difficulty of this kingdom like it's a — like it's a test.

lines.speaker: refugee
lines.text: The reports were filed under a name I'd never seen. Not the chancellor's name. Something else. Something that started with C.

herbalist.description: Village herbalist. Practical healer. Knows the forest's rhythms better than anyone.

lines.speaker: herbalist
lines.text: The corruption changes the plants. Not just kills them — changes them. I found a healing herb yesterday that heals backwards. Took damage to use it. Fascinating, in a deeply upsetting way.

lines.speaker: herbalist
lines.text: I can sell you proper remedies. The kind that heal in the correct direction. You'll want them for the deep forest.

lines.speaker: herbalist
lines.text: You're collecting everything, aren't you? Every herb, every story, every scrap of information this village has. I've met your type. Thorough to the point of compulsion.

lines.speaker: herbalist
lines.text: Here. A recipe I've been working on. The corruption twisted a moonpetal into something that reverses poison — takes the venom out of the blood and puts it into the next thing you hit. Disgusting. Effective.
